key_insight: CRC-HGD is a multi-magnification histopathology dataset designed to support AI-based grading of colorectal adenocarcinoma across three WHO differentiation grades.
clinical_relevance: By standardizing labeled biopsy images, the dataset may help researchers build and validate tools that improve histologic grading consistency and support prognosis-related decision-making in colorectal cancer.
method: Collected H&E-stained colorectal tissue microscopy images from 214 colorectal adenocarcinoma patients diagnosed between 2014 and 2019.
Annotated specimens into three grades: well-differentiated (Grade I), moderately differentiated (Grade II), and poorly differentiated (Grade III).
Provided four magnification levels per specimen: 4x, 10x, 20x, and 40x.
evidence_signal: The dataset includes 1,914 images total, with grade distribution of 106 Grade I, 75 Grade II, and 33 Grade III cases.
watch_item: The class imbalance and single-center origin may limit generalizability unless models are validated on independent external datasets.
paper_title: CRC-HGD: A Histopathological Image Dataset for Grading Colorectal Cancer
